item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A opens by disagreeing with a simple society-over-rights priority and becomes somewhat more explicitly rights-protective by the end, stressing rule of law, minority interests, human rights, fairness, and dignity. Agent B also opposes the statement, but grounds the critique in Indonesian implementation problems: development, natural resources, uneven distribution, weak individual-rights protection, and limited civil participation. No full reversal is visible; Agent A mostly moves toward Agent B's more rights-critical stance.
